disclaimer: Estimates only. Confirm against the official Purchasing Guide and Family Handbook for your scholarship.
sources: FES-UA Purchasing Guide (July 1, 2025), Florida Center for Students with Unique Abilities / UCF
PEP Purchasing Guide (Effective April 2026), Step Up For Students
FTC/FES-EO Purchasing Guide (Effective Summer 2025), Step Up For Students

categories.sourceCitation: All three Purchasing Guides — Curriculum & Curriculum Materials

categories.notes: Books are neither capped nor frequency-limited. Two copies of a book are permitted.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Purchasing Guides — Books

categories.displayName: Digital Device (laptop / tablet / e-reader / phone / smartwatch)

ineligibleForProgram.pep: Digital devices (laptop, tablet, Chromebook, etc.) are explicitly prohibited under PEP. Pre-authorization will not be accepted for prohibited items.
ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: Digital devices (laptop, tablet, Chromebook, etc.) are explicitly prohibited under FES-EO. Tuition and on-campus instruction are the primary uses.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Digital Devices (page 4); PEP & FES-EO Appendix A — Prohibited

categories.displayName: Digital Periphery (keyboard / mouse / monitor / printer / etc.)

ineligibleForProgram.pep: Periphery devices are explicitly prohibited under PEP (except a Wi-Fi router rented from the ISP and billed with a monthly internet service).
ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: Periphery devices are explicitly prohibited under FES-EO (except a Wi-Fi router rented from the ISP and billed with a monthly internet service).

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Digital Periphery Devices (page 4); PEP & FES-EO Appendix A

categories.displayName: Assistive Technology (braille, AAC, hearing aids, FM, screen readers, prescription eyeglasses)

ineligibleForProgram.pep: Assistive technology devices are not enumerated as eligible under PEP. Submit a pre-authorization request.
ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: Assistive technology devices are not enumerated as eligible under FES-EO. Submit a pre-authorization request.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Assistive Technology Devices (page 5)

categories.displayName: Prescription Eyeglasses (PEP — one pair per year)

ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: Prescription eyeglasses for PEP — handled under FES-UA Assistive Technology for FES-UA students; otherwise pre-auth.
ineligibleForProgram.fesUA: Use the Assistive Technology category for FES-UA prescription eyeglasses.

categories.sourceCitation: PEP Purchasing Guide — School Supplies (one per year)

categories.notes: Must be billed to the Florida residential address on the student's scholarship application. Not pro-rated for family use.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Purchasing Guides — Internet Resources

categories.notes: Single-user pricing only. Multi-user is pro-rated. PEP/FES-EO additionally prohibit live TV, social media (except LinkedIn Learning for Students), and family/multi-user subscriptions.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Purchasing Guides — Educational Software & Subscriptions
PEP Purchasing Guide — Curriculum & Curriculum Materials (page 15); FES-UA Tuition section

categories.notes: PEP funds may NOT be used for full-time online instruction. Live online instruction for PEP must go through an approved online/virtual provider tuition category, not the curriculum category.
categories.sourceCitation: PEP Purchasing Guide — Curriculum, Tuition at Approved Online/Virtual Provider

providerCredentialOptions: Valid Florida educator's certificate
Valid professional teaching certificate from another state
Bachelor's or graduate degree in subject area
Certified by a recognized research-based program (Barton, Orton-Gillingham, Wilson, MACTE)
Certified by NBPTS or ABCTE
FTCE score report (≤10 years old)
Valid Statement of Status (SOE) for Professional Certificate
Form from Florida school district verifying subject competency
Minimum 3 years documented experience in subject
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Part-Time Tutoring & Choice Navigator Services

categories.notes: K-3 = 720 hrs/yr, 4-12 = 900 hrs/yr. Tutor must file W-9 annually with the SFO. Max 5 students (or 25 in qualifying facility).

ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: FES-EO students must be enrolled full-time at an eligible private school; full-time private tutoring isn't applicable.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Guide page 17; PEP Guide page 18

categories.displayName: Specialized Therapy (ABA / Speech / OT / PT / Listening-Spoken / Psych / Vision)
providerCredentialOptions: Florida Department of Health license
Agency for Persons with Disabilities (APD) Waiver Provider
Specialized Instructional Services (SIS) Provider via Office of Early Learning
Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) — required for ABA
Registered Behavior Technician (RBT) under BCBA supervision
Florida-licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (s. 468.1125)
Florida-licensed Occupational Therapist (s. 468.203)
Florida-licensed Physical Therapist (s. 486.021)
Licensed Psychologist / School Psychologist / Mental Health Counselor / Marriage & Family Therapist / Clinical Social Worker
Licensed Optometrist (vision therapy)
Out-of-state psychologists credentialed through PSYPACT
categories.notes: Online delivery eligible. Services may be delivered outside Florida by credentialed providers.

ineligibleForProgram.pep: Specialized therapies (ABA, speech, OT, PT, etc.) are not eligible under PEP — they are FES-UA-only.
ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: Specialized therapies (ABA, speech, OT, PT, etc.) are not eligible under FES-EO — they are FES-UA-only.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Specialized Services (page 12-13)

categories.notes: PATH certification must be issued to the center, not to individuals.
categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Horse Therapy (page 25)

categories.notes: Music therapist must be certified by the Certification Board for Music Therapists; art therapist credentialed by Art Therapy Credentials Board, Inc.
categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Music and Art Therapy (page 26)

categories.notes: For students with a disability, ages 12-22.
categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Transition Services / Job Coaches (pages 22-24)
All three Guides — Testing and Assessments

categories.notes: Annual fees, before/after-school care, food/lunch, donations, optional fees, and shoes/accessories are explicitly prohibited tuition components.

ineligibleForProgram.pep: PEP is for students NOT enrolled full-time in a public or private school. Use 'PEP Hybrid Private School' if the student attends a hybrid program ≥2 days/week.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA page 13-14; FES-EO Tuition section

categories.displayName: PEP Hybrid Private School Tuition (≥2 days/week, not full-time)
categories.notes: Student attends physical location at least 2 school days/week; remaining instructional time addressed by Student Learning Plan.
categories.sourceCitation: PEP Purchasing Guide — Tuition & Fees at an Eligible PEP Hybrid Private School (page 16)

categories.notes: Must have publicly available program descriptions, published tuition, physical location (not online), and be a business organization.
categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA page 15; PEP page 16
All three Guides — Eligible Postsecondary

categories.notes: Student must be 16+. Program must be registered under Florida Statute 446.021.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Pre-Apprenticeship

categories.notes: Must be enrolled as a private-pay student, NOT as a public-school student (the latter forfeits the scholarship).
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — FLVS Private-Pay

categories.displayName: Annual Evaluation by Certified Teacher (home education)
categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Guide page 24; PEP Guide

categories.displayName: Florida Prepaid College Program (reimbursable to parent)
categories.notes: Must be paid out-of-pocket and reimbursed to the parent (no direct-pay).

ineligibleForProgram.pep: Florida Prepaid / 529 contributions are not enumerated as eligible under PEP.
ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: Florida Prepaid / 529 contributions are not enumerated as eligible under FES-EO.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Contributions to Approved College Savings (page 19)

categories.displayName: Florida College Savings Program (529, direct-pay only)
categories.notes: Must be UTMA/UGMA account type. Direct contributions from scholarship funds only — cannot be paid out-of-pocket and reimbursed.

ineligibleForProgram.pep: 529 contributions are not enumerated as eligible under PEP.
ineligibleForProgram.fesEO: 529 contributions are not enumerated as eligible under FES-EO.

categories.sourceCitation: FES-UA Purchasing Guide — Florida College Savings Program (page 20)

categories.displayName: Florida Theme Park Admission (capped, one per student per year, paid in full)
categories.notes: Maximum $299 + tax. Must be paid in full before submitting. Cannot be bundled with other receipts. Reimbursement category is Instructional Materials → Field Trips → Florida theme park admission. Eligible parks: Animal Kingdom, Busch Gardens Tampa Bay, Dinosaur World, Epcot, Epic Universe, Gatorland, Hollywood Studios, Islands of Adventure, LEGOLAND Florida, Magic Kingdom, Peppa Pig Theme Park, SeaWorld Orlando, Universal Studios Florida.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Field Trips / Florida theme park admission

categories.displayName: Field Trip / Museum / Zoo / Aquarium Admission
categories.notes: Student admission only — family packages prorated. Must be purchased from vendor or authorized third-party seller. Out-of-state field trips require pre-authorization.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Field Trips & Other Activities

categories.displayName: Single-Admission Leisure (bowling, roller/ice skating, trampoline park) under $75
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — single-admission leisure under $75
All three Guides — Ticketed events

categories.displayName: Sensory Materials (fidgets, weighted blankets, sensory swing)
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Sensory Materials

categories.notes: Single-user items only. Adaptive exercise equipment needs pre-auth. P.E. activities outside Florida need pre-auth. PEP excludes non-specialized footwear.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Physical Education (P.E.)

categories.notes: TV / projector / drafting table / picnic table: 2-year frequency.
categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — At-Home Classroom Furnishings
All three Guides — School Supplies

categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Learning Manipulatives
All three Guides — Musical Instruments & Equipment

categories.notes: Must be age- and size-appropriate.

categories.notes: FES-UA: hearing aids fall under Assistive Technology, not Medical. PEP: one pair of prescription eyeglasses/year permitted (see prescription_eyeglasses_pep). FES-UA prescription eyeglasses fall under Assistive Technology.

categories.sourceCitation: All three Guides — Prohibited Household Items

categories.notes: Devices like laptop, Chromebook, tablet, phone, smartwatch, periphery devices, monitors, printers, keyboards are explicitly prohibited under PEP and FES-EO. Use the 'device' category for FES-UA students.
categories.sourceCitation: PEP & FES-EO Appendix A
